mission: Define the canonical event namespace, schema registry, normalization pipeline, and evolution process so every Vecells lifecycle, continuity, recovery, observability, and audit event resolves through one governed event authority.

canonicalEnvelope.privacyLaw: Raw PHI, phone numbers, message bodies, transcripts, and artifact contents are forbidden; governed refs or masked descriptors only.
canonicalEnvelope.artifactLaw: PHI-bearing payloads require payloadArtifactRef and payloadHash rather than inline raw content.
canonicalEnvelope.replayLaw: Replay consumers share one canonical event contract and prove compatibility through schemaVersionRef plus contract hash.
canonicalEnvelope.evolutionLaw: namespace_break is the only legal way to retire a family in place; otherwise evolve additively or publish a new version with replay proof.

registryDefects.title: External outcomes now publish canonical adapter events
registryDefects.summary: Provider confirmations, delivery receipts, and telephony callbacks are normalized into communication, confirmation, or telephony contracts before downstream use.

registryDefects.title: Object-store artifacts now enter the event spine
registryDefects.summary: Artifact ingress and quarantine are represented by request and intake canonical events instead of orphaned storage side effects.

registryDefects.title: Attachment quarantine contract published
registryDefects.summary: The registry now publishes intake.attachment.quarantined with a stable schema and reason-code payload.

registryDefects.title: Degraded receipt contract published
registryDefects.summary: The registry now publishes patient.receipt.degraded so degraded acknowledgements remain replayable and auditable.

registryDefects.title: Fallback review lifecycle normalized into exception.*
registryDefects.summary: Fallback review cases now publish exception.review_case.opened and exception.review_case.recovered as canonical contracts.

registryDefects.title: Identity repair lifecycle published
registryDefects.summary: Identity repair open, corrected, and closed events are now first-class canonical contracts.

registryDefects.title: Duplicate review lifecycle published
registryDefects.summary: request.duplicate.review_required and request.duplicate.resolved are published and tied to duplicate-review truth.

registryDefects.title: Reachability failure and repair events published
registryDefects.summary: Reachability dependencies now publish created, failed, and repaired transitions as canonical recovery events.

registryDefects.title: Confirmation-gate lifecycle published
registryDefects.summary: External confirmation ambiguity now publishes created, confirmed, and disputed gate events with normalization rules for vendor aliases.

registryDefects.title: Closure blocker change event published
registryDefects.summary: request.closure_blockers.changed is now the canonical blocker-set mutation signal for projections and assurance.

registryDefects.title: Telephony namespace has no dedicated seq_043 runtime producer yet
registryDefects.summary: The registry is published and validator-backed, but telephony events currently rely on future service scaffolding rather than a named telephony runtime executable.

registryDefects.title: Assistive namespace is contract-complete but runtime-reserved
registryDefects.summary: Assistive events are published, but seq_046 still reserves the assistive runtime as a lab surface rather than a canonical service binding.

registryDefects.title: Analytics and audit remain contract-first before dedicated runtime bindings
registryDefects.summary: analytics.* and audit.* are now canonical contracts, but dedicated runtime executables are not yet scaffolded in the validated service set.
